title: DegenDetector: Symbolic Recovery of Parameter Degeneracies in Bayesian Posteriors
headline: DegenDetector: Symbolic Identification of Parameter Degeneracies in Bayesian Posteriors
tldr: DegenDetector automates the discovery of degenerate relationships in posterior distributions by combining mutual information screening with alternating symbolic regression, producing interpretable closed‑form equations. This framework eliminates the need for domain‑specific input, enabling researchers to uncover hidden parameter correlations efficiently.
significance: It provides a systematic, interpretable method to detect and characterize degeneracies in Bayesian inference, improving model diagnostics and interpretability.

title: Phase-dependent kink collisions and dual critical-velocity branches in the complex sine-Gordon model
headline: Phase-Dependent Kink Collisions Reveal Dual Critical Velocities in Complex Sine-Gordon Model
tldr: We numerically study collisions of complex kinks in the CSG model, showing that the final state depends on both initial velocity and relative phase. Unlike the real sine-Gordon case, the CSG system displays two distinct critical velocity branches leading to elastic or inelastic outcomes.
significance: This work uncovers how internal phase degrees of freedom can fundamentally alter soliton interactions, providing new insights into integrable field theories.

title: AUTOPILOT VQA: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models for Incident-Centric Dashcam Understanding
headline: AUTOPILOT VQA: Benchmarking Vision‑Language Models for Incident‑Centric Dashcam Understanding in Autonomous Driving
tldr: We introduce AUTOPILOT‑VQ, a benchmark that evaluates vision‑language models on safety‑critical dashcam scenarios. The dataset and evaluation protocol reveal gaps in current models’ reasoning about incidents, guiding future research.
significance: This benchmark highlights the need for more robust multimodal reasoning in autonomous driving systems, driving progress toward safer vehicles.

title: ARDY: Autoregressive Diffusion with Hybrid Representation for Interactive Human Motion Generation
headline: ARDY: Real-Time Human Motion Generation via Autoregressive Diffusion and Hybrid Representation
tldr: ARDY introduces a hybrid autoregressive diffusion model that generates realistic 3D human motions in real-time, balancing speed and control. It combines text and kinematic constraints with efficient inference, outperforming prior offline and online methods in interactive applications.
significance: This work enables high-fidelity, controllable motion synthesis for animation, robotics, and simulation, bridging the gap between offline precision and online speed.

title: Workflow as Knowledge: Semantic Persistence for LLM-Mediated Workflows
headline: Reimagining LLM Workflows: A Lisp-Inspired Model for Semantic Persistence and Knowledge Sharing
tldr: This paper introduces a language-independent conceptual framework for LLM-mediated workflows, drawing on Lisp-inspired symbolic forms, object identity, and live-image thinking to model knowledge persistence. It argues that these abstractions can guide implementation without dictating specific technologies, enabling more robust, explainable, and reusable workflow systems.
significance: By framing workflow as a persistent knowledge representation, the work offers a principled foundation for building more transparent and adaptable LLM-driven automation.

title: Sculptable Mesh Structures for Room-Scale Form-Finding
headline: From Paper to 3D: Sculptable Mesh Structures for Room-Scale Form-Finding
tldr: Designers often rely on low-fidelity prototypes before finalizing a structure, but this process can be limited by screen-based design. Sculptable mesh structures enable rapid iteration in physical space, bridging the gap between digital concepts and real-world forms.
significance: This approach accelerates the design cycle and improves the integration of aesthetic and functional requirements in built environments.

title: Learning Adaptive Solvers for Distributed Factor Graph Optimization on Matrix Lie Groups
headline: DeepCORD: Learning‑Augmented Distributed Factor Graph Optimization on Matrix Lie Groups
tldr: DeepCORD introduces a learning‑augmented framework that enables robust, distributed factor graph optimization across general matrix Lie groups, eliminating brittle hand‑tuning. It extends beyond rigid‑body pose graphs, improving scalability for multi‑robot perception tasks.
significance: By integrating deep learning with distributed optimization, DeepCORD offers a scalable, adaptable solution for complex geometric inference in robotics, overcoming limitations of traditional solvers.

title: The Illusion of Equivalency: Statistical Characterization of Quantization Effects in LLMs
headline: Quantization Misleads: New Metric Reveals Hidden LLM Behavior Changes
tldr: Post‑training quantization is common for deploying large language models, but accuracy and perplexity miss key behavioral shifts. Introducing correctness agreement, a decision‑level metric, better captures overlap in correct predictions between a base model and its quantized counterpart.
significance: This work highlights that conventional metrics can mask quantization‑induced behavior changes, offering a more reliable evaluation tool for deploying LLMs.

title: Super Weights in LLMs and the Failure of Selective Training
headline: Super Weights in LLMs: Pruning Degrades Performance, Yet Targeted Training Fails
tldr: Recent research shows that removing a small set of critical parameters—Super Weights—can drastically reduce LLM performance, but surprisingly, training only these parameters does not improve the model. This suggests that the importance of Super Weights is context-dependent and that selective training strategies may not generalize across architectures.
significance: This finding challenges the assumption that fine-tuning critical parameters alone can recover or enhance LLM capabilities, indicating a need for more holistic training approaches.

title: Validity of LLMs as data annotators: AMALIA on authority
headline: Portugal’s AMALIA Model Matches Human Coders on Moral Authority Classification
tldr: Portugal’s publicly funded 9B-parameter AMALIA model demonstrates strong performance in coding the moral foundation of authority, matching trained human coders within six F1 points. Compared to larger open models, AMALIA shows competitive agreement despite being significantly smaller.
significance: This shows that regionally tailored LLMs can achieve high annotation accuracy, enabling more precise linguistic and cultural analysis.

title: WaspMOT: A Benchmark for Long-Term Multi-Object Tracking of Trichogramma Wasps
headline: Introducing WaspMOT: A Long-Term Multi-Object Tracking Benchmark for Trichogramma Wasps
tldr: WaspMOT provides a long-duration video dataset for tracking Trichogramma wasps, addressing the gap in identity preservation over extended periods. The benchmark enables evaluation of tracking algorithms in controlled ecological settings, encouraging development of robust long-term MOT methods.
significance: By focusing on long-term identity tracking, WaspMOT pushes the field toward more reliable and persistent object tracking solutions.

title: Cataclysmic Variables Photometric Periods from TESS
headline: TESS Uncovers 1,362 Periodic Cataclysmic Variables in 1,557 Stars
tldr: Using TESS data up to sector 102, researchers identified stable photometric periods in 1,362 cataclysmic variables, including eclipsing systems, polars, and intermediate polars. The study also presents the first period determinations for several previously uncharacterized objects.
significance: This work expands the catalog of known periodic cataclysmic variables, providing a valuable resource for studying binary evolution and accretion physics.

title: Wat3R: Underwater 3D Geometry Learning without Annotations
headline: Wat3R: Semi-Supervised Underwater 3D Geometry Learning Without Annotations
tldr: Wat3R introduces a cross-domain semi-supervised framework that learns 3D geometry from underwater imagery without dense annotations. By leveraging synthetic-to-real domain adaptation and self-supervised cues, it achieves accurate 3D reconstructions in challenging aquatic scenes.
significance: This work enables scalable 3D mapping of underwater environments, overcoming the scarcity of annotated data and paving the way for autonomous marine exploration.

title: OPSD-V: On-Policy Self-Distillation for Post-Training Few-Step Autoregressive Video Generators
headline: OPSD-V: On-Policy Self-Distillation Enhances Long-Horizon Video Generation
tldr: OPSD-V introduces an on-policy self-distillation framework that refines few-step autoregressive video diffusion models after training, mitigating error accumulation over long sequences. The method preserves low-latency inference while significantly improving motion fidelity and reducing degradation in extended video rollouts.
significance: It enables high-quality long videos from efficient few-step AR diffusion models, bridging the gap between speed and fidelity.

title: Typicality of Steering for Two-qubit States
headline: Assessing Typicality of Quantum Steering in Two-Qubit States: A Statistical Exploration
tldr: We investigate how often two-qubit states exhibit quantum steering by sampling random states and computing steering measures. The results reveal a high prevalence of steering, challenging assumptions about its rarity and informing resource theories.
significance: This study quantifies steering typicality, providing insights for quantum communication protocols and foundational tests of nonlocality.

title: Hockey stick $f$-divergences
headline: Unified Treatment of Quantum Hockey Stick f-Divergences for Non-Normalized States
tldr: This paper presents a systematic framework for quantum hockey stick f-divergences, extending previous results to non-normalized states. It also introduces new generalizations and provides unified proofs for key properties.
significance: It advances the mathematical foundation of quantum information theory by providing a versatile divergence measure applicable to a broader class of quantum states.

title: MulTTiPop: A Multitrack Transcription Dataset for Pop Music
headline: MulTTiPop: A 3.5-Hour Benchmark Dataset for Multitrack Pop Music Transcription
tldr: MulTTiPop offers 572 pop music segments with multitrack MIDI annotations, covering 1930s-2000s genres. It enables evaluation of automatic music transcription models on diverse, real-world pop tracks.
significance: The dataset provides a comprehensive, open-source benchmark for advancing automatic music transcription research.

title: SLORR: Simple and Efficient In-Training Low-Rank Regularization
headline: SLORR: A Lightweight In-Training Low‑Rank Regularizer for Efficient Neural Network Compression
tldr: SLORR introduces a simple low‑rank regularization technique that can be applied during training without costly SVDs or extra parameters. It achieves comparable compression rates to state‑of‑the‑art methods while maintaining model accuracy and enabling real‑time deployment.
significance: SLORR offers a practical, low‑overhead solution for compressing modern neural networks, making them more deployable on resource‑constrained devices.

title: Finding Simple Proofs for First-Order Optimization
headline: Towards Transparent, Reusable Proofs in First-Order Optimization: A New Approach
tldr: This work proposes a method to generate simple, interpretable proofs for first-order optimization problems, replacing dense certificates with reusable structures. It leverages performance estimation techniques to produce transparent proofs that can be checked and reused by researchers.
significance: It bridges the gap between automated proof certification and human-readable, reusable proof structures, advancing mathematical rigor in optimization.

title: DexVerse: A Modular Benchmark for Multi-Task, Multi-Embodiment Dexterous Manipulation
headline: DexVerse: Modular Benchmark Evaluating General-Purpose Dexterous Manipulation Across Tasks and Embodiments
tldr: DexVerse introduces a modular benchmark that systematically tests dexterous manipulation policies across multiple tasks, sensory conditions, and robot embodiments. By providing diverse data and controllable visual variation, it enables robust cross-task and cross-embodiment evaluation, addressing gaps in current benchmarks.
significance: DexVerse fills a critical gap by offering a comprehensive, open-source platform that facilitates the development and comparison of general-purpose dexterous manipulation policies.

title: Debris Disc Substructures Induced by Secular Planetary Perturbations
headline: Unveiling Planetary Influence on Debris Disc Structures Through Secular Perturbation Analysis
tldr: We analyze how an inner planet's secular gravitational perturbations shape the azimuthally-averaged surface density of a debris disc. Our analytical framework predicts observable substructures that can reveal hidden planetary companions.
significance: This work provides a new diagnostic tool for detecting unseen planets by interpreting disc substructures in astronomical observations.

title: Deep Spectroscopic Follow-Up of Maisie's Galaxy -- A Typical Galaxy in the Early Universe
headline: JWST Deep Spectroscopy Reveals Typical Star-Forming Galaxy at z>10
tldr: Using 19+ hours of NIRSpec G395M and MIRI/LRS data, astronomers confirm that a bright z>10 galaxy exhibits normal star formation rather than extreme activity. This challenges assumptions that all early bright galaxies are AGN-dominated.
significance: The study shows that many early-universe galaxies may follow standard evolutionary paths, refining models of cosmic star formation.

title: Using AI-based Learning Assistants in Higher Education: A Large-Scale Descriptive Analysis
headline: Large-Scale Study Reveals How Students Use AI Learning Assistants Across Demographics
tldr: This research analyzes log data from 77,543 distance-learning students to uncover usage patterns of the AI assistant Syntea. Findings show significant differences in engagement across gender, age, study cluster, degree, and mode, highlighting the need for tailored support.
significance: The study provides the most comprehensive evidence to date on chatbot engagement in higher education, informing design and policy.

title: Dimensionality Reduction Meets Network Science: Sensemaking on UMAP's kNN Graph
headline: Uncovering Hidden Insights: Using UMAP's kNN Graph for Advanced Data Sensemaking
tldr: UMAP's kNN graph holds rich manifold information before dimensionality reduction. By analyzing this graph, researchers can gain deeper insights into high-dimensional data structure.
significance: This approach unlocks new analytical possibilities beyond traditional UMAP embeddings, enabling more accurate data interpretation.

title: ZipDepth: Bringing Lightweight Zero-Shot Monocular Depth Anywhere, on Any Device
headline: ZipDepth: Lightweight Zero‑Shot Monocular Depth for Any Device
tldr: ZipDepth delivers foundation‑model quality depth estimation on embedded and mobile hardware by combining lightweight architecture with robust zero‑shot generalization. It outperforms existing single‑domain models and works across diverse scenes without retraining.
significance: This work enables real‑time depth perception on resource‑constrained devices, opening new possibilities for mobile robotics, AR, and remote sensing.

title: LongE2V: Long-Horizon Event-based Video Reconstruction, Prediction, and Frame Interpolation with Video Diffusion Models
headline: LongE2V: Using Video Diffusion Models for Long-Horizon Event-Based Video Reconstruction
tldr: LongE2V fine-tunes pre-trained video diffusion priors to reconstruct, predict, and interpolate frames from sparse event streams, achieving high-quality results without the blur common in regression methods. The approach demonstrates stable long-term performance, surpassing existing generative models.
significance: This work enables realistic, temporally coherent video generation from minimal event data, opening new possibilities for low-light and high-speed vision applications.

title: Geometry and Gradient-based Partitioning for Panoramic Outdoor Reconstruction
headline: Efficient 3D Reconstruction of Outdoor Scenes Using Panoramic Images and Gradient Partitioning
tldr: This work introduces a geometry- and gradient-based partitioning method that enables scalable 3D Gaussian splatting for large outdoor scenes captured with 360° panoramic images. By replacing local camera frustum partitioning with a global visibility-aware scheme, the approach reduces data acquisition effort and computational cost while maintaining reconstruction quality.
significance: It demonstrates that panoramic imagery can be leveraged for efficient, high-fidelity outdoor 3D reconstruction without the heavy computational overhead of traditional block-wise optimization.

title: UniClawBench: A Universal Benchmark for Proactive Agents on Real-World Tasks
headline: UniClawBench: Universal Benchmark for Proactive Agents across Real-World Tasks and Environments
tldr: UniClawBench introduces a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating proactive agents in realistic, multi-turn scenarios, moving beyond sandboxed, single-turn tests. It incorporates diverse real-world tasks to assess agents’ planning, execution, and adaptability.
significance: By providing a realistic, multi-turn evaluation framework, UniClawBench enables more accurate assessment of proactive agents’ practical capabilities, guiding future model development.

title: Plaquette: A hardware-aware design platform for fault-tolerant quantum computers
headline: Plaquette: Hardware-Aware Design Platform Accelerates Fault-Tolerant Quantum Computer Development for Real-World Noise
tldr: Plaquette is a software platform that incorporates realistic hardware noise models to evaluate fault-tolerant quantum architectures. It enables designers to identify which imperfections to suppress and guides hardware teams toward more efficient, noise-aware quantum computer designs.
significance: By bridging the gap between theoretical error models and actual device noise, Plaquette accelerates the development of scalable, fault-tolerant quantum processors.

title: Enhancing In-context Panoramic Generation via Geometric-aware Pretraining
headline: Canvas360: Geometry-Aware Pretraining and 1M-Sample Dataset for High-Quality In-Context Panoramic Generation
tldr: We propose Canvas360, a two-stage framework that first pretrains a diffusion model on a large 1M-pair panoramic dataset, then fine-tunes it for specific tasks such as style transfer and inpainting. The resulting model achieves state-of-the-art quality while enabling efficient in-context generation.
significance: This work provides the first large-scale panoramic dataset and a geometry-aware pretraining strategy that dramatically improves the realism and versatility of in-context panoramic generation.

title: OpenCoF: Learning to Reason Through Video Generation
headline: OpenCoF: Harnessing Video Generation for Temporal Reasoning in AI Models
tldr: OpenCoF introduces Chain-of-Frame reasoning, enabling large models to derive logical conclusions through temporally linked video frames. By training on diverse video datasets, it expands reasoning beyond static text, offering a new paradigm for AI decision-making.
significance: This approach bridges the gap between visual dynamics and logical inference, paving the way for more robust AI systems that can reason over time.

title: Irreducible Geometry of Higher-Order Correlator Families
headline: Unveiling the Geometry of High-Order Correlators in Programmable Quantum Simulators
tldr: Programmable quantum simulators now measure complex many-body correlators, but interpreting them lacks a solid theoretical framework. This paper introduces a geometric perspective on higher-order correlator families, offering new tools for analysis.
significance: By providing a geometric framework, the study bridges the gap between experimental data and theoretical understanding, enabling deeper insights into quantum dynamics.

title: Measuring Consumption with Credit Card Data: Benchmarking and Beyond
headline: Credit Card Spending Reveals County-Level Consumption Trends, Matching Traditional Measures
tldr: We built a monthly county-level consumption dataset from 350M credit card transactions, covering 3,000+ U.S. counties since 2014. The data explain 92% of the variation in monthly PCE growth, offering a high-frequency benchmark for economic analysis.
significance: This dataset provides a near real-time, high-resolution proxy for consumer spending, enabling more timely and granular economic insights.

title: Ideas Have Genomes: Benchmarking Scientific Lineage Reasoning and Lineage-Grounded Idea Generation
headline: IdeaGene-Bench: Measuring AI's Ability to Trace Scientific Lineage
tldr: Scientific ideas build on prior work, but current AI benchmarks don't test this inheritance. IdeaGene-Bench evaluates whether language models can reason about scientific lineage and generate ideas grounded in that lineage.
significance: This benchmark pushes AI research toward more biologically inspired, cumulative scientific discovery.

title: Score Accuracy Along the Forward Diffusion Does Not Certify Numerical Stability in Diffusion Sampling
headline: Small Forward-Marginal Error Does Not Ensure Stable Diffusion Sampling in Reverse-Time
tldr: The study shows that controlling average error under forward marginals does not guarantee numerical stability of reverse-time samplers. By constructing a smooth score field with arbitrarily small forward-marginal L² error, the authors demonstrate that the learned reverse-time process can remain nonexplosive yet still exhibit instability.
significance: This work highlights the inadequacy of forward-marginal error as a sole metric for diffusion model stability, urging the development of more robust training and evaluation criteria.

title: Accurate, Interdisciplinary and Transparent Structure-property Understanding with Deep Native Structural Reasoning
headline: Deep Native Structural Reasoning for Interdisciplinary Structure‑Property Insight
tldr: This work introduces a framework that leverages large language models to interpret spatial, chemical, and periodic structural data, enabling mechanistic explanations across biology, chemistry, and materials science. By integrating stereochemistry, bonding, and symmetry, the approach offers transparent, data‑driven insights into function and reactivity.
significance: It bridges fundamental science with AI, providing a scalable, interpretable tool for predicting material behavior and guiding experimental design.

title: The Subversive Role of Excessive External Shear in Concealing Lensing Anomalies
headline: External Shear’s Hidden Influence: How Overestimated Shear Masks Gravitational Lensing Anomalies
tldr: Gravitational lens models often overestimate external shear, which can conceal subtle lensing anomalies. This study shows that relying solely on image positions leads to biased shear estimates, masking the true mass distribution.
significance: Accurate shear estimation is crucial for interpreting lensing data and probing dark matter substructure.

title: Co-LMLM: Continuous-Query Limited Memory Language Models
headline: Continuous-Query Limited Memory Language Models: Externalizing Knowledge for Dynamic Retrieval
tldr: Limited Memory Language Models (LMLMs) store factual knowledge in an external knowledge base instead of in model weights, enabling on-demand retrieval during generation. The proposed continuous-query approach enhances knowledge control and adaptability beyond traditional LLMs.
significance: This paradigm shifts how language models manage knowledge, offering greater control and scalability for real-world applications.

title: The Key to Going Linear: Analysis-Driven Transformer Linearization
headline: Linearizing Transformers: A Data‑Driven Approach to Efficient Long‑Context Attention Inference
tldr: This study demonstrates that carefully designed state‑update mechanisms can replace softmax in causal self‑attention without sacrificing quality. By freezing the backbone and focusing on rank‑1 orthogonal projections, the authors achieve linear complexity while preserving performance on long‑context tasks.
significance: It offers a principled, post‑hoc linearization method that can accelerate large language models for real‑time applications.

title: Radio emission from close-in exoplanets: Can we extend the radio-magnetic scaling law to the sub-Alfvénic stellar wind regime?
headline: Extending Exoplanet Radio-Magnetic Scaling to Sub-Alfvénic Stellar Winds: New Insights
tldr: Scientists are testing whether the radio-magnetic scaling law, which predicts radio aurora from exoplanets, holds in the sub-Alfvénic regime of close-in planets. Early results suggest the law may need adjustment, opening a new window into star‑planet magnetic interactions.
significance: Confirming radio emission from close‑in exoplanets would provide a direct method to measure their magnetic fields, crucial for understanding planetary habitability.

title: Coherent Signal Detection with Pruning -- I. Finding Short-Period Binary Pulsars in Circular Orbits
headline: Efficient Coherent Signal Detection for Short-Period Binary Pulsars Using Pruning Techniques
tldr: The paper introduces a pruning-based algorithm for coherent detection of pulsars in short-period binary orbits, significantly reducing computational cost. It demonstrates improved sensitivity over constant-acceleration searches while maintaining real-time feasibility.
significance: This method enables deeper and faster searches for binary pulsars, opening new avenues for testing general relativity.

title: Mitigating the Winner's Curse While Controlling Multiplicity: e-Process Methods for Anytime-Valid Inference in Dose-Ranging Trials
headline: E-Process Approach to Control Winner's Curse in Dose-Ranging Trials
tldr: This paper introduces an anytime-valid e-process framework that simultaneously addresses the winner's curse and multiplicity in Phase II dose-ranging trials. By testing the best true dose effect while allowing continuous monitoring, the method controls Type I error without sacrificing power.
significance: It provides a statistically rigorous solution for unbiased inference in adaptive dose-finding studies, improving decision-making in early drug development.

title: Induced Erdős--Pósa property for long holes, long thetas, and beyond
headline: Induced Erdős–Pósa Property for Long Holes and Thetas in Graphs
tldr: This paper establishes the induced Erdős–Pósa property for long cycles and long theta graphs as induced minors. It shows that the maximum number of pairwise anti‑adjacent copies of these structures is bounded by a function of the minimum hitting set size, extending classical results to induced settings.
significance: It extends the Erdős–Pósa theory to induced subgraph containment, providing new insights into the structure of long cycles and theta graphs in sparse graphs.

title: Breaking Database Lock-in: Agentic Regeneration of High Performance Storage Readers for Database Bypass
headline: Breaking Database Lock‑In: Agentic Storage Readers Bypass JDBC for High‑Performance Analytics
tldr: Jailbreak bypasses traditional database drivers by directly reading storage files, enabling bulk columnar analytics without engine overhead. This approach unlocks high‑performance, low‑latency data access for analytical workloads.
significance: It eliminates the performance bottleneck imposed by database drivers, allowing analytics engines to operate directly on storage, dramatically improving throughput and reducing costs.

title: Institutional Red-Teaming: Deployment Rules, Not Just Models, Causally Shape Multi-Agent AI Safety
headline: Institutional Red‑Teaming: Rules Shape Multi‑Agent AI Safety
tldr: We propose institutional red‑teaming, a method that isolates deployment rules to assess their causal impact on multi‑agent AI behavior. Using IABench‑CA, we evaluate five canonical rules across 228 contexts with seven models.
significance: This work demonstrates that deployment rules, not just models, fundamentally influence AI safety outcomes.

title: Selective Timestep Weighting and Advantage-Based Replay for Sample-Efficient Diffusion RLHF
headline: Efficient Diffusion RLHF via Selective Timestep Weighting and Advantage Replay
tldr: The paper introduces a method that selectively weights diffusion timesteps and uses advantage-based replay to reduce the number of human or reward model queries needed for RLHF. Experiments show significant sample efficiency gains over prior diffusion RLHF approaches.
significance: It makes aligning diffusion models with human preferences more practical by cutting feedback costs.

title: Faster quantum linear system solver beyond the condition number
headline: Quantum Linear Solver Beats Condition Number Bottleneck
tldr: Two new quantum algorithms solve Ax=b with runtime independent of the condition number, achieving accuracy ε with improved complexity over traditional methods. They dramatically reduce the resource overhead for typical problem instances.
significance: This breakthrough lowers quantum resource demands for linear systems, bringing practical quantum advantage closer to reality.

title: Agon: Competitive Cross-Model RL with Implicit Rival Grading of Reasoning
headline: Agon: Two Models Compete to Grade Each Other’s Reasoning in Reinforcement Learning
tldr: Agon introduces a novel competitive framework where two reinforcement learning agents simultaneously solve the same problem and evaluate each other’s reasoning traces, providing richer feedback than final-answer grading. This implicit rival grading encourages models to produce more accurate, verifiable reasoning steps, improving overall problem-solving performance.
significance: By turning models into mutual graders, Agon addresses the lack of intermediate reasoning evaluation, pushing the frontier of trustworthy AI reasoning.

title: Agent Delivery Engineering Predictive Reliability Framework
headline: Enhancing Reliability of Long-Horizon LLM Multi-Agent Systems Through Proactive Health Trajectory Prediction
tldr: The ADE-PRF aggregates 20 heterogeneous signals across five layers to compute a Trust Margin metric, enabling proactive health trajectory prediction for long-horizon LLM multi-agent systems. By detecting passive degradation early, it mitigates invisible reliability risks that conventional infrastructure monitoring misses.
significance: This framework provides a systematic, data-driven approach to anticipate and prevent reliability failures in complex AI systems, improving operational resilience.

title: Skipping the rungs! Calibrating distance indicators through their clustering with galaxies
headline: Calibrating Cosmic Distance Indicators via Galaxy Clustering Cross-Correlations for Improved Cosmological Parameter Estimation
tldr: We demonstrate that angular cross-correlations between distance indicators and galaxy redshift surveys can detect calibration biases in distance measurements. By modeling a constant magnitude offset, we show how clustering statistics constrain this systematic and improve cosmological inference.
significance: This method offers a novel, model-dependent way to quantify and correct distance calibration errors, enhancing the precision of cosmological studies.

title: ELSA3D: Elastic Semantic Anchoring for Unified 3D Understanding and Generation
headline: ELSA3D: Elastic Semantic Anchoring Enhances Unified 3D Understanding and Generation
tldr: ELSA3D introduces elastic semantic anchoring to decouple text and 3D tokens, preserving structural cues and fine geometry in a unified foundation model. This approach improves language-3D interaction and generation quality over flat concatenation methods.
significance: By separating semantic and geometric representations, ELSA3D advances the fidelity and interpretability of 3D foundation models, enabling more accurate language-driven 3D content creation.

title: Lift3D-VLA: Lifting VLA Models to 3D Geometry and Dynamics-Aware Manipulation
headline: Lift3D-VLA: Enhancing Vision‑Language Models with 3D Geometry for Real‑World Manipulation
tldr: Lift3D-VLA augments Vision‑Language‑Action models with 3D point‑cloud data and dynamic reasoning, enabling more accurate spatial understanding for robotic manipulation. By integrating geometry‑aware modules, the framework overcomes data scarcity and preserves spatial detail, paving the way for robust real‑world applications.
significance: This work bridges the gap between language‑driven AI and geometric perception, unlocking safer, more precise robot interactions in complex environments.

title: Minimum Energies and Magnetic Field Strengths of Edge-brightened Compact Symmetric Objects
headline: Estimating Minimum Energies and Magnetic Fields in Edge-Brightened Compact Symmetric Objects
tldr: Using radio surveys and VLBA imaging, the study derives the minimum energy requirements and magnetic field strengths for edge-brightened CSOs, shedding light on jet launching mechanisms. The results provide a benchmark for future high-resolution radio studies of young radio galaxies.
significance: The work offers crucial constraints on the energetics of nascent radio jets, informing models of AGN feedback and galaxy evolution.

title: Vision as Unified Multimodal Generation
headline: Unified Multimodal Vision: SenseNova-Vision Uses Language to Drive Image Tasks
tldr: SenseNova-Vision reframes diverse computer vision tasks as multimodal generation, eliminating the need for task‑specific models. By leveraging natural‑language instructions and optional visual prompts, it specifies tasks, target regions, and decoding strategies within a single unified framework.
significance: This approach streamlines vision pipelines, enabling flexible, language‑driven task specification across a wide range of visual problems.

title: RynnWorld-4D: 4D Embodied World Models for Robotic Manipulation
headline: RynnWorld-4D: Leveraging RGB-DF for Real-Time 4D World Modeling in Robotics
tldr: RynnWorld-4D introduces RGB-DF, a synchronized RGB, depth, and optical flow representation that captures 4D scene dynamics for robotic manipulation. This multi-modal approach outperforms 2D videos by aligning visual cues with physical motion, enabling more accurate interaction planning.
significance: By integrating RGB, depth, and flow, RynnWorld-4D provides a physically grounded 4D model that enhances robotic manipulation in open-world environments.

title: ProxyPose: 6-DoF Pose Tracking via Video-to-Video Translation
headline: ProxyPose: Accurate 6-DoF Pose Tracking Using Video-to-Video Translation Without Extra Data
tldr: ProxyPose introduces a novel video-to-video translation framework that estimates 6-DoF pose of objects and surfaces from monocular video alone, eliminating the need for 3D models, depth maps, or masks. The method demonstrates robust performance on textureless, reflective, and deformable surfaces, achieving state-of-the-art accuracy in real-time scenarios.
significance: By removing the dependency on auxiliary inputs, ProxyPose enables practical, real-time pose estimation in challenging visual environments, broadening the applicability of monocular vision systems.

title: The Thick Part of the $\mathrm{PSL}_n(\mathbb{R})$-Hitchin-Riemann Moduli Space has Infinite Volume
headline: Infinite Volume of Thick Part in PSL_n Hitchin Moduli Space
tldr: We prove that for n>2 the thick part of the PSL_n(R)-Hitchin moduli space has infinite Atiyah–Bott–Goldman volume. This contrasts with Mumford’s compactness criterion and is achieved using Goldman flows and internal sequences.
significance: This result challenges the expectation of compactness in moduli spaces, revealing richer geometric structure.

title: From RGB Generation to Dense Field Readout: Pixel-Space Dense Prediction with Text-to-Image Models
headline: Leveraging RGB Generation for Dense Field Prediction with Text-to-Image Models
tldr: Large-scale text-to-image models trained on RGB generation encode rich semantic and geometric priors that can be repurposed for dense prediction tasks. By mapping depth, normals, and segmentation masks into the VAE latent space, the approach enables pixel-space dense readout without retraining from scratch.
significance: This method unlocks efficient, high-fidelity dense prediction by reusing pre-trained generative backbones, reducing data and compute requirements.

title: MonoIR-RS: Infrared Remote Sensing Vision-Language Learning with CLIP and VLM Adaptation
headline: MonoIR-RS: Advancing Infrared Remote‑Sensing Vision‑Language Models
tldr: MonoIR‑RS introduces a large‑scale infrared remote‑sensing vision‑language dataset and adapts CLIP and VLMs for infrared imagery, enabling robust cross‑modal understanding. The benchmark demonstrates significant gains over RGB‑only models on tasks such as object detection and semantic segmentation in infrared imagery.
significance: It fills a critical gap by providing the first comprehensive infrared vision‑language resource, paving the way for more accurate remote‑sensing analytics in low‑visibility conditions.

title: Quantum combinatorial games
headline: Exploring Quantum Advantage in Combinatorial Games: A New Frontier in Game Theory
tldr: This paper extends classical combinatorial game theory into the quantum domain, redefining game rules and strategies. It demonstrates scenarios where quantum players outperform classical opponents, revealing surprising advantages.
significance: The work opens new avenues for quantum algorithm design and deepens our understanding of strategic interactions under quantum mechanics.
